gate_info.recommendation: Demo aquifer parameterization (K=0.0001 m/s, porosity=0.3) — NOT site-specific hydrogeology. Confirm to run the MODFLOW groundwater-plume simulation for trichloroethylene near Twin Falls, Idaho.

narration.scroll_text_tail:
nt of Water Resources warned
that even a partial infiltration of the 12,000-gallon load could establish a
long-lived contaminant plume migrating with the regional groundwater gradient.

"TCE doesn't break down quickly, and it sinks," said a department spokesperson.
"Once it reaches saturated sands and gravels, it can travel a long way from the
release point. Our first job is to understand how far and how fast."

The driver was treated for minor injuries and released. No residents were
reported hurt, though officials advised three nearby households on private wells
to switch to bottled water as a precaution while monitoring wells are installed.
Crews worked through the night to excavate the most heavily saturated soil, but
state officials cautioned that some fraction of the solvent had almost certainly
reached the water table by the time containment berms were in place.

The Eastern Snake River Plain aquifer supplies irrigation water to hundreds of
farms across south-central Idaho and feeds the springs that discharge into the
Snake River canyon near Twin Falls. A contaminant plume in this setting could
take years to characterize and far longer to remediate.

Federal and state agencies opened a joint investigation into the cause of the
crash and the integrity of the tanker's containment system. A full delineation
of the subsurface plume — its likely footprint and peak concentration — is
expected to take weeks of monitoring-well sampling and groundwater-transport
modeling. Officials said modeling the plume's probable extent is the immediate
priority so that downgradient wells can be prioritized for sampling.completed: list_categoriescompleted: list_tools_in_categorycompleted: run_model_groundwater_contamination_scenarioI've modeled the groundwater contamination plume from the reported TCE spill near Twin Falls, Idaho. Based on the article, I extracted the following parameters for the simulation:

Contaminant: Trichloroethylene
Location: Twin Falls, Idaho
Spill Volume: 12,000 gallons
Release Duration: 6 hours

The simulation shows a plume with a peak concentration of 2946.3 mg/L covering an area of 0.0125 km². The plume layer has been added to the map for visualization.
Please note that this model uses generalized aquifer parameters (hydraulic conductivity of 0.0001 m/s and porosity of 0.3) as the article does not provide site-specific hydrogeologic data. The actual plume may vary depending on the real-world properties of the Eastern Snake River Plain aquifer.
